but they can't get any results and people that actually know how to promote. They know how to write effective emails. They know how to follow the sequence. They know how to close. ## [06:06 - 09:07]

I've promoted for something and I've actually not gotten paid. So they just have to trust you as a person and you as a business that you are going to take care of them and pay them. Then they're actually going to be interested in. This goes along with making sales. What's the metrics on the dollars per visit? So for every visitor, for every click they send you, how much are they going to make? Or it could be dollars per opt-in. So hopefully you've done that internal launch already and you know what the numbers were for your internal launch. Now the reality is they'll get, every partner will get a different dollars per visit and dollars per opt-in. And they'll probably be different and probably be lower than for your internal launch, but this is, they're going to be doing this kind of math. Whether you can give them these numbers or not, they're going to be looking at it and say, well, if I send them 100 clicks or 1,000 clicks or 10,000 clicks, how much am I going to be able to make? So next they want to, they want to know that you've got a great product and that you stand behind it. And this means you just deliver a great product, you've got a great guarantee because again, they're putting their relationship with their list at risk on some level because they're recommending you. They're saying, go check this out. So if they're saying, go check it out, it's like you tell a friend, hey, go watch this movie and then they go to that movie and they actually hate the movie, then they're not going to trust your movie recommendations. So that's not that big a deal on a friendship because there'll be like, well, Jeff's just got really bad taste in movies or I don't agree with his taste. That's fine. But if I say, go buy this product to my list and it's a lousy product, then my list is not going to believe me the next time and they're going to not feel quite so good about me and our relationship. So that's really important. Another thing they might be looking at as a partner is what we call reciprocity. So this is like, if they promote for your launch or your product, will you turn around and mail for them? That happens a lot. They're like, okay, I'll mail for you if you mail for me. So they might be looking at possible reciprocity and if they're looking at that, then they're going to wonder about how powerful your list is, how big your list is, how, what kind of results because it's, if there's like a reciprocity between, you know, here's, here's partner A and here's partner B and partner A says, I'll mail for you if you mail for me. So I'm going to mail for you if you mail for me, but this person sends 100,000 clicks, but this person can only send 100 clicks, then A is just not going to be all that excited about the reciprocity angle.
